The '''Star Forge Robes''' were special [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]] that were made in an ancient massive automated [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]], the [[Star Forge]]. These [[Robe/Legends|robes]] were long, [[Color/Legends|colored]] sand-brown, and only its chest plate, gloves, and boots broke the ascetic simplicity. Nonetheless, it benefited a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] wearer by increasing their abilities. Despite the fact that the Star Forge was a powerful tool of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], only one immersed in the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] was able to wear the robes.<ref name="kotor" /> In [[3956 BBY]], during the [[Battle of Rakata Prime|final battle]] of the [[Jedi Civil War]],<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] [[Battle of Rakata Prime|defeated]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Lord]] [[Darth Malak]] and the Star Forge was destroyed, setting an end to the production of the robes.<ref name="kotor" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Star Forge Robe first appeared in [[BioWare]]'s RPG [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', released in [[July]] [[2003]]. Star Forge Robes can be created at one of the computer rooms on the Star Forge for a large number of [[Computer spike/Legends|computer spikes]]. The robe gave the wearer an increase in their wisdom and saving throws. In the non-canonical dark side storyline of the game, these robes cannot be created. Instead, a different set of robes would be created, mimicking but not matching [[Darth Revan's robes|Revan's]] original [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] robes.<ref name="kotor" />
